assumptions: The question relates to consolidated financial statements.

The dividend receivable is a recognised intragroup monetary item between group entities with different functional currencies, and the related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised foreign-currency intragroup receivable that creates profit-or-loss exposure in consolidation is relevant to assess under the fair value hedge model.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The foreign-currency settlement amount of a recognised receivable can be relevant to assess under the cash flow hedge model when the variability affects profit or loss.
